activities_projects.title: Identify the Speakers
activities_projects.objective: Understand the speakers in the poem
steps: Read the poem carefully
Identify who is speaking in each stanza
Write their roles in 2–3 sentences
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Identifies the speakers and their viewpoints

activities_projects.title: Meaning of Key Lines
activities_projects.objective: Understand important lines of the poem
steps: Select two important lines from the poem
Write their meaning in your own words

activities_projects.title: Hair Colour Discussion
activities_projects.objective: Understand the central idea of appearance vs inner self
steps: Identify the colour of Anne Gregory’s hair
Write what she says she can change it to
Explain why she says this

activities_projects.title: Poet Information
steps: Read the note about William Butler Yeats
Write 3–4 lines about his life and achievements

activities_projects.title: Theme of the Poem
steps: Think about the message of the poem
Write 3–4 sentences describing its theme

activities_projects.title: Vocabulary from the Poem
steps: Find five new words from the poem
Write their meanings
Use each word in a sentence

activities_projects.title: Personal Opinion
steps: Think about whether appearance is important in liking someone
Write 3–4 sentences giving your opinion

activities_projects.title: Poem Summary
steps: Read the poem again
Write a short summary in 4–5 sentences

activities_projects.title: Moral Message
activities_projects.objective: Understand the deeper message of the poem
steps: Think about what the poem teaches about love and appearance
Write the moral message in 2–3 sentences

activities_projects.title: Creative Illustration
steps: Draw a simple illustration representing the poem
Add a caption explaining your drawing
